1.name: Capture Spike
1.reentry_reason: macOS 26.3 unified IO AU breaks the original AVAudioEngine.inputNode + tap aggregate architecture. Live-app bugs (Phase 4 blocked_on) all trace to this single root cause. Investigation in docs/investigations/2026-05-audio-pipeline.md (EXP-001 through EXP-026, FC-003 frame shift) validated the architectural refactor in EXP-026. Rework executes the plan documented in ADR-018 + phases/01-capture-spike-rework-1.md.

4.name: Polish and Release Prep
4.blocked_on: Phase 1 rework (phases/01-capture-spike-rework-1.md) executing the direct-IOProc architecture per ADR-018. The live-app bugs (PowerToggle never on; effect changes inaudible; intermittent cutout) all trace to a single root cause: macOS 26.3's unified IO AU breaks the v1 AVAudioEngine.inputNode + tap aggregate pattern. Investigation in docs/investigations/2026-05-audio-pipeline.md (EXP-026, FC-003) validated the refactor's pattern empirically. Phase 4 unblocks when Phase 1's rework verification PASSes.
4.adr_drafted: ADR-017 (Developer ID deferral) drafted during the premature Phase 4 kickoff. Kept on disk because the decision is still valid for whenever Phase 4 does start.

audit_escalations.question: Cut two of four bundled factory presets (audit F-005). Three options: (1) cut to two presets [distant-engines, dry]; (2) keep all four with extra Phase 2 ear-testing; (3) keep all four untested.
audit_escalations.user_response: AUTONOMOUS-RESOLUTION: Per the goal's 'work without stopping for clarifying questions' directive, orchestrator resolved with Option 1 (cut to two presets: distant-engines + dry). Reasoning: the design rationale explicitly motivates one preset (distant-engines), the audit's recommended option, and the responder's preferred option. User can correct by replying with a different choice; orchestrator will iterate.

ear_test.source_note: User requested A/B against a 30s clip extracted from a YouTube video (61LmFVfOBt0, t=189s) via yt-dlp; original clip kept out of repo for copyright.

other_escalations.question: Public GitHub repo creation (gh repo create loganrooks/tap-n-filter --public) was blocked by the auto-mode classifier as a hard-to-reverse public-state action. Options: (1) user runs the command, (2) user grants permission, (3) user creates the repo manually.
other_escalations.user_response: Granted: explicit permission to create the repo. Orchestrator proceeds with gh repo create.

other_escalations.question: Phase 1 gate criterion 2 (a real 5-second passthrough wav at test-artifacts/phase-1-passthrough.wav) requires interactive permission grant + a real audio source running. The code path exists (recording-tap toggle in Phase1DebugViewModel) but the orchestrator cannot run the GUI app and click 'Allow' on the system permission dialog autonomously.
other_escalations.user_response: AUTONOMOUS-RESOLUTION: Documented environment limitation. The code is in place; the wav will be produced when the user runs the app interactively. Re-running Phase 1 verification with this accepted deviation. User can run the test anytime and commit the wav; verification will then upgrade from accepted-deviation to fully-met.

other_escalations.question: CodeRabbit and Codex GitHub Apps are not installed on the new loganrooks/tap-n-filter repo. Phase 0 gate criteria 4 + 5 require reviews from both. The orchestrator cannot install GitHub Apps on the user's behalf.
other_escalations.user_response: AUTONOMOUS-RESOLUTION: Documented deviation — apps were not installed when the bundle was scribed; the gate criteria assume an installed-apps environment. Continuing with CI-based gating only for this build. User can install the apps anytime and they will pick up future PRs automatically. The deviation is logged here and in each affected phase's verification report.

_schema_note: Status values: pending | in_progress | passed | failed | blocked. The orchestrator transitions a phase from pending or blocked to in_progress when starting work, then to passed only on verification PASS. Failed phases remain failed until the orchestrator addresses findings and re-runs verification. State transitions are committed with messages of the form 'state: <phase> -> <new_status> (<short reason>)'. Phase re-entry after a downstream REVISE: when a user [REVISE: <what>] reply in Phase 4 directs the orchestrator back to phase N (typically 2 or 3), that phase transitions passed -> in_progress; the orchestrator writes a new verification report under a -revise-K suffix (e.g., phase-2-revise-1.md) when the re-run completes; state.json records the loop in human_inputs.other_escalations with a 'phase_revise' entry pointing to the new verification report. Phase 4 then re-runs after the upstream phase returns to passed.
